fundamentals.name: Datadog, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Datadog, Inc. operates an observability and security platform for cloud applications in the United States and internationally. The company's products comprise infrastructure and application performance monitoring, log management, observability pipelines, synthetics, real user monitoring, product analytics, continuous profiler, database monitoring, data observability, LLM observability, error tracking, network monitoring, incident response, workflow automation and App builder, event management, bits AI SRE, cloud cost management, cloud security, code security, cloud SIEM, threat management, sensitive data scanner, and CI visibility. Datadog, Inc. was incorporated in 2010 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong revenue and earnings expansion, but thin operating margins and a very high valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 18.3%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 32.2% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 113.4% indicate strong business momentum.
Gross margin of 79.9% is exceptionally high, suggesting strong pricing power.
Free cash flow of $936.73M TTM demonstrates solid cash generation.
Analyst consensus is 'strong_buy' with a mean target above the current price.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 572.85 and Price/Sales of 22.21 indicate a very high valuation.
Operating margin of 0.8% and Profit margin of 3.7% are thin relative to gross margin.
Beta of 1.29 suggests higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: Datadog (DDOG) is seeing mixed analyst sentiment, with JPMorgan maintaining a bullish outlook and raising its target, while Goldman Sachs raised its target but kept a sell rating. Recent reports highlight the company's strong performance in AI production environments and its first $1 billion quarter, with analysts pointing to its benefits as AI adoption grows. Despite a high trailing P/E, the company shows strong revenue and earnings growth, though operating margins remain thin.
